---
title: " "
page-layout: full
toc: false
number-sections: false
---

::::: {.ap-layout}

:::: {.ap-sidebar}

::: {.ap-profile-card}

![](images/portrait.png){.ap-avatar-wrap style="display:block;margin:0 auto 1.1rem;"}

<p class="ap-profile-name">Ahmed M. Elhossiny</p>
<p class="ap-profile-role">Postdoctoral Fellow</p>
<p class="ap-profile-role">Computational Oncology</p>
<p class="ap-profile-role">Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center</p>

:::

<ul class="ap-social-links">
<li><a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/ahmedelhossiny93/" target="_blank"><i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i> LinkedIn</a></li>
<li><a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=R76GOmMAAAAJ&hl=en" target="_blank"><i class="bi bi-mortarboard-fill"></i> Google Scholar</a></li>
<li><a href="mailto:elhossa1@mskcc.org"><i class="bi bi-envelope-fill"></i> elhossa1@mskcc.org</a></li>
<li><a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0884-8754" target="_blank"><i class="bi bi-person-badge-fill"></i> ORCID</a></li>
<li><a href="pipelines/index.qmd"><i class="bi bi-diagram-3-fill"></i> Code/Workflows</a></li>
<li><a href="articles/index.qmd"><i class="bi bi-journal-text"></i> Articles</a></li>
</ul>

::::

:::: {.ap-content}

## About

My research career is defined by a commitment to bridging computational oncology and experimental biology to accelerate the development of translatable therapeutic strategies. Trained as a pharmacist, I developed an early appreciation for the full arc of drug development—from foundational biological discovery to direct patient benefit.

I pursued a PhD in Bioinformatics at the University of Michigan to build the statistical and computational foundation needed to extract meaningful biology from large-scale, complex data. Under interdisciplinary mentorship at Michigan, Johns Hopkins, and Memorial Sloan Kettering, I am focusing on dissecting epithelial and stromal co-evolution in cancer using single-cell and spatial transcriptomics.

<!-- ## Research Interests

::: {.interest-tags}
[Tumor microenvironment]{.interest-tag}
[Lineage plasticity]{.interest-tag}
[Single-cell & spatial multi-omics]{.interest-tag}
[Cancer evolution]{.interest-tag}
[Neuroendocrine carcinoma]{.interest-tag}
[ML for genomics]{.interest-tag}
::: -->

## Education

- **PhD · Bioinformatics** — University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI (2020–2025)
- **MS · Genetics, Genomics & Bioinformatics** — State University of New York at Buffalo, NY (2018–2020)
- **BPharm · Pharmacy** — Ain Shams University, Cairo, Egypt (2011–2016)

## Awards & Fellowships

- **Rackham Predoctoral Fellowship** · University of Michigan (2024–2025)
- **Rackham International Student Fellowship** · University of Michigan (2021)
- **Fulbright Scholarship** · Egyptian Student Program (2018–2020)
- **BPharm with Excellent Grade & High Honors** · Ain Shams University (2016)

## Selected Publications

<ul class="pub-list">

<li class="pub-item">
<span class="pub-title">Asynchronous evolution of epithelium and stroma differentiates precursor lesions from pancreatic cancer </span>
<span class="pub-authors"><strong>Elhossiny AM</strong>, Kadiyala P, Okoye JO, et al.</span>
<span class="pub-venue">Cancer Discovery, 2026</span>
</li>

<li class="pub-item">
<span class="pub-title">Analysis of Donor Pancreata Defines the Transcriptomic Signature and Microenvironment of Early Neoplastic Lesions</span>
<span class="pub-authors">Carpenter ES*, <strong>Elhossiny AM</strong>*, Kadiyala P*, et al.</span>
<span class="pub-venue">Cancer Discovery, 2023</span>
</li>

<li class="pub-item">
<span class="pub-title">Oncogenic and tumor-suppressive forces converge on a progenitor-orchestrated niche to shape early tumorigenesis</span>
<span class="pub-authors">Reyes J, Del Priore I, …, <strong>Elhossiny AM</strong>, …, Lowe SW</span>
<span class="pub-venue">Cell, 2026</span>
</li>

<li class="pub-item">
<span class="pub-title">Immune heterogeneity in small-cell lung cancer and vulnerability to immune checkpoint blockade</span>
<span class="pub-authors">Nabet BY, …, <strong>Elhossiny AM</strong>, …, Shames DS</span>
<span class="pub-venue">Cancer Cell, 2024</span>
</li>

<li class="pub-item">
<span class="pub-title">Immunocompetent Murine Models Recapitulate the Heterogeneous Tumor-Immune Microenvironment of Human Liposarcoma</span>
<span class="pub-authors">Shafer AM, …, <strong>Elhossiny AM</strong>, …, Angeles CV</span>
<span class="pub-venue">Clinical Cancer Research, 2025</span>
</li>

<li class="pub-item">
<span class="pub-title">Proximogram — A multi-omics network-based framework to capture tissue heterogeneity</span>
<span class="pub-authors">Krishnan SN, Ji S, <strong>Elhossiny AM</strong>, et al.</span>
<span class="pub-venue">Computers in Biology and Medicine, 2024</span>
</li>

</ul>


<!-- ## Skills

::: {.skill-tags}
[R]{.skill-tag}
[Python]{.skill-tag}
[Seurat]{.skill-tag}
[Scanpy]{.skill-tag}
[Nextflow]{.skill-tag}
[PyTorch]{.skill-tag}
[Bash]{.skill-tag}
[HPC/Slurm]{.skill-tag}
[scRNA-seq]{.skill-tag}
[Spatial transcriptomics]{.skill-tag}
[WES]{.skill-tag}
::: -->

::::

:::::
